novel_features.name: Visibility weekly delta
novel_features.description: See exactly what moved in AI Visibility week over week — overview, trend, sentiment, or traffic — without screenshotting two dashboard tabs and diffing them by hand.
novel_features.rationale: The SurgeGraph UI shows point-in-time snapshots, not deltas. Requires two snapshots in local SQLite and a row-wise diff that no single API call returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the user asks what changed in AI Visibility or wants a movers report; it answers without burning a follow-up API roundtrip.

novel_features.name: Prompts that lost AI mentions
novel_features.description: Surface tracked prompts whose citation count or first-position rank dropped vs the prior window — the report a CMO asks for every Monday and the dashboard can't produce.
novel_features.rationale: Joins ai_visibility_prompts with date-bucketed daily runs in the local store; the API exposes the snapshots but not the comparison.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the user is hunting for visibility regressions; gives the agent a ranked, actionable list instead of two raw snapshots to compare.

novel_features.name: Citation-domain rank shift
novel_features.description: Watch which domains gained or lost citation share for your project's AI-tracked prompts over a 30-day window — useful when a competitor starts crowding the answer set.
novel_features.rationale: Diffs two snapshots of ai_visibility_citations grouped by domain in the local store. No API equivalent.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when monitoring competitive share-of-voice in AI answers.

novel_features.name: Knowledge-library impact
novel_features.description: Rank a project's knowledge libraries by how often their grounded URLs actually appear in tracked AI citations — answers "is this library worth maintaining?".
novel_features.rationale: Joins knowledge_library_documents.url against ai_visibility_citations.url. Crosses two product surfaces the API exposes separately.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the user audits knowledge libraries or asks which library is paying off.
novel_features.group: Cross-entity joins no single API call returns

novel_features.name: Topic-coverage drift
novel_features.description: Diff a topic_research's hierarchical map against the writer_documents you've already shipped — covered vs gap, ranked for action.
novel_features.rationale: Joins topic_research nodes against writer_documents.title via FTS. The topic_research API returns the tree; the docs API returns titles; no endpoint joins them.
novel_features.example: surgegraph-pp-cli research drift --research-id res_xyz789 --project proj_abc123 --agent --select topic,parent,covered
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when planning a content sprint from a topic research; tells the agent exactly which subtopics still need articles.
novel_features.group: Cross-entity joins no single API call returns

novel_features.name: Gap to publish pipeline
novel_features.description: Take topic-gap output, bulk-create documents in 50-doc batches, and queue them into a WordPress integration — one idempotent command for the content-ops ritual.
novel_features.rationale: Compounds get_topic_map, create_bulk_documents, and publish_document_to_cms with retry-safe idempotency. Today this is three tabs and a spreadsheet.
novel_features.example: surgegraph-pp-cli research gaps publish --research-id res_xyz789 --project proj_abc123 --integration wp_int_456 --dry-run --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when the user wants to ship articles for every gap from a topic research; collapses a multi-tab manual workflow into one call.

novel_features.name: Stale-doc projection
novel_features.description: List writer and optimized docs not updated in the last N days, ranked by recent AI-source traffic — the refresh queue in one query.
novel_features.rationale: Local query over writer_documents.updated_at joined with ai_visibility_traffic_pages. Ranks by impact, not just age.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when prioritizing a content refresh queue.

novel_features.name: Competitor topic diff
novel_features.description: Set-diff a competitor's domain_research topic tree against your own topic_research — instant view of what they cover and you don't.
novel_features.rationale: Joins two locally cached research trees. The web app forces a mental side-by-side; the join is local-only.
novel_features.example: surgegraph-pp-cli research domain diff --project proj_abc123 --mine res_xyz789 --theirs dom_res_aaa --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when comparing topical coverage against a named competitor.
novel_features.group: Cross-entity joins no single API call returns

novel_features.name: Cross-project visibility roll-up
novel_features.description: Roll up AI Visibility deltas across every project in the organization — the agency-portfolio view the single-project UI doesn't produce.
novel_features.rationale: Fans the delta engine across every project in the local store. There is no cross-project API endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user manages multiple projects (agency, multi-brand) and asks for an executive summary.

novel_features.name: Quota burn-down forecast
novel_features.description: Project credit exhaustion from your last 30 days of usage snapshots — a pre-flight check before queueing a large bulk-publish run.
novel_features.rationale: Linear projection over locally cached get_usage snapshots; the live endpoint shows current balance, not trajectory.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this before kicking off a bulk operation to confirm you have credits.

novel_features.name: Traffic-page to citation join
novel_features.description: For each top-traffic page, list the tracked prompts whose AI citations resolved to it — answers "why is this page getting AI traffic?".
novel_features.rationale: Joins ai_visibility_traffic_pages.url against ai_visibility_citations.url; the API exposes both lists but never their join.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when explaining traffic patterns or planning new content based on what's already pulling AI clicks.
novel_features.group: Cross-entity joins no single API call returns

novel_features.name: Agent context bundle
novel_features.description: Emit a JSON blob shaped for an agent's context window covering prompts, citations, docs, and topic-maps for one project — the handoff payload an agent's loop needs.
novel_features.rationale: Reads ≤5 entity tables from local SQLite, emits one structured JSON. Reproducible without re-fetching the API for every agent run.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to seed another agent's working memory with everything you know about a SurgeGraph project, in one round-trip.

novel_features.name: Local search
novel_features.description: Full-text search across local cache: AI Visibility prompts, citations, writer documents, optimized documents, and topic-map nodes — one query, multi-entity hits.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite FTS5 index across multiple tables. The API has no unified search; the web UI has none across these entities.
novel_features.example: surgegraph-pp-cli search "AI search optimization" --kind prompts,citations,docs,topics --agent --select kind,id,title,snippet
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when an agent needs to locate any signal — a prompt, a citation, a draft — across the whole local cache.

novel_features.name: Sync diff
novel_features.description: Emit per-resource sync cursors and total row counts from the local store — the inspection primitive for agent loops that need to know whether a re-sync is due.
novel_features.rationale: Reads per-resource `last_synced_at` cursors recorded by `sync` and emits a structured snapshot of what is cached. The `--since` flag is reserved for a future incremental-diff implementation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run after `sync` to inspect cursor freshness, or in an agent loop to decide whether the local store needs to be re-synced before reading.

narrative.headline: Every SurgeGraph workflow — research, write, publish, monitor — from the terminal or any agent, with a local SQLite that compounds week-over-week deltas the web app can't produce.
narrative.value_prop: SurgeGraph is an AI-search content operations cockpit. The web app shows point-in-time snapshots; this CLI keeps a local cache so you can run `visibility delta`, `prompts losers`, and `citation-domains rank-shift` over time. It compounds three split workflows — topic research, bulk writing, WordPress publishing — into a single `research gaps publish` pipeline, and ships every command as both CLI and MCP so the same agent that drafts an article can monitor the citations it generates.
narrative.auth_narrative: SurgeGraph uses OAuth 2.1 against https://mcp.surgegraph.io with Authorization Code + PKCE + Dynamic Client Registration. Run `surgegraph-pp-cli auth login` once; the CLI registers a client, walks you through the browser flow, and stores the bearer plus refresh token for subsequent calls. `auth status` shows the current TTL and `auth logout` clears it.

quickstart.comment: OAuth 2.1 + PKCE handshake against mcp.surgegraph.io; tokens cached locally.
Confirm bearer, quota, and tool reachability before doing anything else.
Pick a project_id; every visibility/docs/knowledge command needs one.
Populate the local store so deltas and search work.
Week-over-week AI Visibility movers — the canonical Monday ritual.

quickstart.command: surgegraph-pp-cli research gaps publish --research-id res_xyz789 --project proj_abc123 --integration wp_int_456 --dry-run
quickstart.comment: Preview the gap-to-WordPress pipeline before letting it actually post.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `surgegraph-pp-cli auth login`. If you were authed, the refresh token expired — `auth status` shows the TTL.
Run `surgegraph-pp-cli sync --project <id>` at least twice on different days; deltas need two snapshots.
Verify with `visibility prompts list --project <id>` that the project tracks any prompts at all. Empty losers list with non-empty prompts means nothing dropped — that's the answer.

troubleshoots.symptom: `research gaps publish` errors out partway through
troubleshoots.fix: It's idempotent on the bulk-create step. Re-run with `--dry-run` first; matching documents are skipped by external_id.
The CLI uses cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter and backs off automatically. If errors persist after retry, run `account burn --window 168h` — you may be near the quota ceiling.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent needs to operate against SurgeGraph for any AI-search content task: monitoring AI Visibility deltas, drafting and publishing articles in bulk, auditing knowledge-library impact, or seeding another agent with a project's current state. Every visible web-app feature is also a CLI command, and every transcendence command is an MCP tool — the agent that opened the loop can close it without leaving the terminal.

recipes.title: Monday AI Visibility review
recipes.explanation: Pull week-over-week movers and pipe straight into the Monday CMO update.

recipes.title: Trim a deeply-nested citations response
recipes.explanation: Citation responses are deeply nested; dotted-path `--select` keeps the payload under an agent's context budget.

recipes.title: Find every signal about a topic across the local cache
recipes.command: surgegraph-pp-cli search "AI search optimization" --kind prompt,citation,doc,topic --agent --select kind,id,title,snippet
recipes.explanation: FTS5 across all four entities in one round-trip; results are unified and rankable. Run `sync` first to populate the index.

recipes.title: Publish every gap topic to WordPress
recipes.command: surgegraph-pp-cli research gaps publish --research-id res_xyz789 --project proj_abc123 --integration wp_int_456 --dry-run
recipes.explanation: Compounds get_topic_map → create_bulk_documents → publish_document_to_cms; idempotent on retries.

recipes.title: Filter results to one citation kind from the local cache
recipes.explanation: Limit FTS hits to one entity kind via `--kind`; combine with `--limit` to keep output bounded.

trigger_phrases: what changed in SurgeGraph this week
publish gap articles from a topic research
what knowledge libraries actually show up in citations
